series_title: ABSTRACT OF THE DUPLICATE OF REAL
series_description: Tax years 1962-1971, Incl. office area. Single Vol. Bound Co. Aud. Form N-15. A record of tax valuation and taxes charged for all purposes on real and public utility property by taxing districts. This record is also filed with the Auditor of State annually, although, the uses and purposes of this office differ. These abstracts when combined with information from personal property abstracts provide the bases for allocation of state moneys to counties under the local government fund. Sec. 5739.22, R.C., and the State Foundation Program. Sec. 3317.10 R.C. The duty providing this information is imposed on the Department of Tax Equalization. Abstracts prior to 1926 were destroyed in Wyandot building fire.
retention_statement: Paper: Current and preceeding year unbound. Next 10 preceeding years bound. Vols. for the tax year to 1961 to be placed in the custody of the Ohio Historical Society the Dept. of Tax Equalization retaining ownership and provision made annually to retire to the Society the earliest volume in our file.

Prior to tax year 1932, real, public utility and personal property of all classes was carried on the same tax list and one record prepared.
retention_statement: Paper: Current and preceeding year unbound. Next 10 preceeding years bound. Vols. for the tax year 1932 to 1961 to be placed in the custody of the Ohio Historical Society with the Dept. of Tax Equalization retaining ownership and provision made annually to retire to the Society the earliest volume in our file.

series_description: The Real Property Conveyance Fee Statement is an affidavit which identifies real estate sold and provides information such as the property's assessed value and selling price used in market studies conducted by the Ohio Department of Taxation for purposes of real property assessment equalization.
retention_statement: Electronic: Current and preceding 3 years retained for counties scheduled for sexennial re-appraissal or triennial update in the current year. For counties not scheduled for re-approaisal or triennial update in the current year, the DTE form 100's on file will be those for the current year and years since the last re-appraisal or update, with no forms being maintained longer than three years prior to the current year. ; Paper: Current and preceding 3 years retained for counties scheduled for sexennial re-appraissal or triennial update in the current year. For counties not scheduled for re-approaisal or triennial update in the current year, the DTE form 100's on file will be those for the current year and years since the last re-appraisal or update, with no forms being maintained longer than three years prior to the current year.

series_description: Journal Entries memorializing final settlement of certified debt or allowance of refund claims by the Tax Commissioner.
retention_statement: Electronic: Destroy 4 years after the end of the calendar year in which the settlement was finalized or the refund was processed. ; Paper: Destroy 4 years after the end of the calendar year in which the settlement was finalized or the refund was processed.

series_description: Any distribution information of municipal income tax revenue to local governments and accompanying reports, worksheets, spreadsheets, settlements and other supporting documentation. Records include local government breakdown of distributions, any and all allocation reports, revenue source data, and taxpayer information associated with each distribution.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years after distribution is made. ; Electronic: Retain for 5 years after distribution is made.

series_description: Information Releases - technical advisories that offer detailed explanations of selected laws, rulings that govern the taxes administered by the Ohio Department of Taxation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Should be kept for ten years from the date the release is rescinded or superseded.

series_description: Records include registration forms or applications and supporting documentation for the Commercial Activity Tax, Petroleum Activity Tax, Financial Institution Tax, Wireless 9-1-1 Charge or Next Generation 9-1-1 Access Fee, and Resort Area Gross Receipts Excise Tax.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 4 years after the account is canceled or closed ; Paper: Retain 4 years after the account is canceled or closed

series_title: Real Property Tax Reduction Factor Reports
series_description: These reports contain the mathematical calculations for real property tax reduction factors required by Ohio statutes. These reports have two or more separate tables of data and calculations: Table "A"; Table "B"; School District Minimum Rate Guarantee Calculations; Joint Vocational School Minimum Gurantee Calculations; Table "C".
retention_statement: Paper: Reports are to be retained for 10 years on paper medium ; Microform: 1994 and prior this information is maintained on microfilm and is kept permanently ; Electronic: After 1995 these reports have been imaged and archived in the computer system.
disposition: Paper: Shred ; Microform: Permanent ; Electronic: Permanent
